zh.Bridgwater, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特布利其沃特
zh.enmore, Somerset, England: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特恩莫
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibilla Saint Clare於大約1268年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特恩莫時，她的父親Robert de Saint Clare25歲，母親Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare21歲。她於1328年之前與Sir John Malet在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1331年在英國薩默塞特恩莫享年64歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特恩莫。

zh-hans.Bridgwater, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特布利其沃特
zh-hans.enmore, Somerset, England: 英国英格兰薩默塞特恩莫
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sibilla Saint Clare于大约1268年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特恩莫时，她的父亲Robert de Saint Clare25岁，母亲Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare21岁。她于1328年之前与Sir John Malet在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1331年在英国薩默塞特恩莫享年64岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特恩莫。

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Sibilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybille
meanings: From Greek. See Sybil .

variants.name: Sibella
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibilla Saint Clare was born about 1268, in Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert de Saint Clare, was 25 and her mother, Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, was 21. She married Sir John Malet before 1328, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1331, in Enmore, Somerset, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Enmore, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sibilla Saint Clare est née environ 1268, à Enmore, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Saint Clare, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Malet avant 1328, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1331, à Enmore, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Enmore, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sibilla Saint Clare um 1268 in Enmore, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Saint Clare 25 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare 21. Sie hat vor 1328 in England Sir John Malet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1331 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Enmore, Somerset, England und wurde in Enmore, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sibilla Saint Clare è nata il circa 1268 nel Enmore, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Robert de Saint Clare, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Malet prima del 1328, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1331, nel Enmore, Somerset, England, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Enmore, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sibilla Saint Clareが約1268年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトエンモーで生まれた時，父Robert de Saint Clareは25歳，母Mrs. Robert de Saint Clareは21歳であった。1328年以前にイギリスでSir John Maletと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1331年にイギリスソメルセトエンモーで64歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトエンモーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sibilla Saint Clare(이)가 대략 1268년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 모어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Saint Clare(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1328년 이전 영국에서 Sir John Malet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1331년 영국 서머세트 모어에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 모어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sibilla Saint Clare nasceu aproximadamente 1268, em Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert de Saint Clare, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Malet antes 1328, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1331, em Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sibilla Saint Clare родилась около 1268 г. в местности энмор, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert de Saint Clare было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Malet перед 1328 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1331 г. в местности энмор, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности энмор, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sibilla Saint Clare nació aproximadamente 1268, en Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert de Saint Clare, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. Robert de Saint Clare, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir John Malet antes de 1328, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1331, en Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Enmore, Somerset, Inglaterra.
